texto: Anger should be especially kept down in punishing, because he who comes to punishment in wrath will never hold that middle course which lies between the too much and the too little. It is also true that it would be desirable that they who hold the office of Judges should be like the laws, which approach punishment not in a spirit of anger but in one of equity.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Johannes Voet: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Anger should be especially kept down in punishing, because he who comes to punishment in wrath wi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que trabalho, anger, cicero pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de trabalho e cicero.

It is also true that it would be desirable that they who hold the office of Judges should be like the laws, which approach punishment not in a spirit of anger but in one of equity.
